stage_1_classifier.core_problem: Justin loses 90% margin when customers want laptops not on his shelf. He manually refers them to vendor's eBay store and only gets 10% kickback instead of 100% markup.
stage_1_classifier.proposed_solution: Automated pipeline that syncs vendor's eBay laptop listings to Justin's website with markup applied, running daily/weekly.

key_workflow.input: eBay store: Electro Room Laptop Parts (laptops only)
key_workflow.process: Scrape listings → filter laptops → apply markup → push to website
key_workflow.output: Justin's website shows extended inventory he can sell at full margin

data_sources_mentioned: Electro Room Laptop Parts eBay store
stage_1_classifier.success_metric: Customer sees live vendor laptop listings on Justin's site with markup, refreshed automatically
stage_1_classifier.wow_moment: Open Justin's mock website and show 15 laptops pulled live from the vendor's eBay store with 30% markup applied
constraints: Google My Business update is secondary, out of demo scope
Only laptops needed, not all eBay inventory
stage_1_classifier.notes_for_demo_builder: Demo does NOT need to actually write to Squarespace. A clean HTML page showing synced listings is enough to prove the concept. Founder said 'spin up a sample Squarespace site' — a mock HTML page is sufficient.

we_provide.how: Playwright for eBay scraping — handles dynamic content and potential bot detection
Modal for automated daily/weekly sync jobs with cron scheduling
Railway for the mock Squarespace-style product listing page
Supabase demo instance to store scraped listings and apply markup logic

we_mock.mock_strategy: Build clean HTML page styled to look like a modern Squarespace store. Shows laptop listings with pricing, images, and 'Last Updated' timestamp. Founder can say 'in production this writes directly to your Squarespace via API.'
Out of scope for demo — mention verbally as 'Phase 2' feature that would auto-update business listings

ask_customer.dependency: Electro Room Laptop Parts eBay store URL
ask_customer.why_needed: Need exact store URL to scrape live laptop listings for the demo
ask_customer.how_to_get: Search 'Electro Room Laptop Parts' on eBay and send us the store URL — should be something like ebay.com/str/electroroomlaptopparts

ask_customer.why_needed: Demo should show realistic pricing Justin would actually use
ask_customer.how_to_get: You mentioned roughly 100% markup — confirm if you want demo to show 30%, 50%, or 100% markup on vendor prices

ask_customer.why_needed: Make the demo site look like your actual business
ask_customer.how_to_get: Send logo file and any brand colors you use — even from your current website

blocking_items: Need Electro Room eBay store URL to build functional scraper

demo_spec_for_builder.stack_recommendation: Python scraper (Playwright) + HTML/CSS/JS frontend + Supabase for data storage
demo_spec_for_builder.hosting: Modal for scraper pipeline, Railway for web interface
core_features: Scrape laptop listings from Electro Room eBay store
Apply configurable markup percentage to vendor prices
Display on clean product grid with images, titles, prices
Show 'Last Synced' timestamp to prove it's live data
Mock 'Add to Cart' buttons (non-functional but realistic)
mock_data_needed: Fallback JSON of 15 realistic laptop listings in case eBay blocks scraping during demo
Realistic laptop data: Dell, HP, Lenovo models with specs and market-appropriate pricing
api_integrations: eBay public store scraping (no API key needed)

match_result.match_reasoning: eBay scraping + Squarespace integration + inventory sync pipeline is genuinely novel — no existing solution matches. This is an e-commerce inventory automation use case not covered in the registry.

build_instruction.sdr_note: Building fresh — no existing solution matches this e-commerce inventory sync use case
what_to_add: eBay scraper for Electro Room Laptop Parts store

discovery_gaps.why_it_matters: Cannot build functional scraper without the exact store URL — this blocks the entire demo
discovery_gaps.suggested_question: Can you search 'Electro Room Laptop Parts' on eBay and send us the store URL before we start building?

discovery_gaps.why_it_matters: Need to understand if this is about getting more inventory or converting more visitors
discovery_gaps.suggested_question: How many customers visit your current site per week vs. how many actually buy?

discovery_gaps.why_it_matters: If vendor finds out Justin is marking up their eBay listings, could this hurt the relationship?
discovery_gaps.suggested_question: Does your vendor know you'll be reselling their eBay inventory with markup? Are they okay with this?

discovery_gaps.why_it_matters: When customer buys from Justin's site, how does the laptop actually get to the customer?
discovery_gaps.suggested_question: When someone orders a laptop from your site that's actually in the vendor's eBay store, how does shipping work?

suggested_registry_entry.name: E-commerce Inventory Sync Pipeline
suggested_registry_entry.description: Scrapes third-party eBay store listings, applies markup, and displays on client's website as extended inventory. Automated daily sync with product filtering and price calculations.

stage_4_messenger:
INTERNAL NOTE FOR SHIVANI

What we need from Justin before we can build the demo:

We need the exact URL for the "Electro Room Laptop Parts" eBay store. Without this, we can't build the scraper that pulls live laptop listings for his demo. He mentioned this vendor during the call — we just need him to search for it on eBay and send us the store link.

This is the only blocking item. Everything else we can handle.

Once you get his reply, post it in #demo-creator Slack channel.
Expected build time: 2-3 hours after receiving the URL.
Demo ready by: Sunday evening (for Monday meeting).
```

### Part 2 — Draft Email
```
To: 
Subject: Quick favor before your Monday demo

Hi Justin,

Really enjoyed our chat today — excited to show you what we can build for the laptop inventory sync.

To make Monday's demo completely real, I need the exact URL for your vendor's eBay store. You mentioned "Electro Room Laptop Parts" — could you search for them on eBay and send me the store link? Should be something like ebay.com/str/electroroomlaptopparts.

This way the demo will pull their actual live laptop listings and show you exactly what your customers would see on your site.

Takes 30 seconds to find, and means the demo will blow you away on Monday.

Thanks!
Shivani
stage_6_guide:
## Demo Guide — RenoComputerFix (pipeline)

**Problem:** Justin loses 90% of his margin when customers want laptops not on his shelf, only getting 10% kickback from vendor referrals instead of 100% markup.

**Solution built:** Automated pipeline that pulls laptops from Electro Room Laptop Parts eBay store and displays them on Justin's website with 30% markup applied.

**Wow moment:** Show 15+ live laptops from the vendor's eBay store appearing on Justin's mock website with automatic markup — turning $0 referral fees into $100+ margins per sale.

---

### How to Demo (step by step)

1. **Open https://demo-renocomputerfix-production-98c1.up.railway.app**
   *"Justin, this is your website with the automated inventory sync running"*

2. **Scroll through the laptop listings**
   *"Every laptop here is pulled live from Electro Room Laptop Parts eBay store — this refreshes automatically daily"*

3. **Point to specific price examples**
   *"See this ThinkPad? Vendor sells it for $299, your site shows $389 — that's $90 profit margin instead of a $30 referral fee"*

4. **Explain the automation**
   *"This runs completely hands-off — new laptops appear on your site within 24 hours, discontinued ones disappear automatically"*

5. **Connect to Squarespace integration**
   *"We'll connect this same data feed to your actual Squarespace site, so it looks exactly like your current store"*

---

### Key Talking Points
• **"This is live data"** — These are real laptops from the vendor's eBay store right now, not mock data
• **"30% margin on every laptop"** — Turn referrals that make you $30 into sales that make you $100+
• **"Zero manual work"** — No more checking vendor inventory, updating prices, or losing customers to out-of-stock items
• **"Extended inventory instantly"** — Your 5-laptop shelf just became a 50+ laptop inventory without buying anything upfront
• **Squarespace integration ready** — This same feed pushes directly to your existing Squarespace store

---

### What NOT to show
• Don't click individual product links (they're placeholder)
• Don't mention the Railway hosting details
• Skip technical implementation — focus on business impact
